p: A reference index for everything named in this corpus — formats, services, standards, libraries, concepts, and the peer OGC stacks worth being aware of. Each entry gives a one-line description and a canonical URL.

ac:rich-text-body.p: In plain terms. the lookup you reach for when a previous chapter mentions a thing and you want the official documentation. Quartz's backlinks panel shows you where each term is used; this page tells you where to learn more.

ol.li: Map clients, libraries, and frontend tools

p: Within each category, entries are alphabetical. Where a canonical URL does not exist (concepts rather than products), the closest authoritative reference is given.

ul.li: Cloud Optimised Point Cloud (COPC) — LAZ point cloud reorganised as a clustered octree with a header-resident index, readable by HTTP byte range.
Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF (COG) — tiled, overview-bearing GeoTIFF designed for partial HTTP range reads from object storage.
FlatGeobuf — binary streamable vector format used as an efficient intermediate before tile generation.
GeoJSON (RFC 7946) — JSON encoding for geographic feature data.
GeoParquet — Apache Parquet with a standardised geometry encoding, enabling predicate-pushdown spatial reads from object storage.
Mapbox Vector Tile (MVT) — protobuf-encoded vector tile format used inside PMTiles archives.
MosaicJSON — descriptor format for virtual mosaics over many COGs, quadkey-indexed.
PMTiles — single-file archive of map tiles indexed for HTTP range reads from object storage.
Zarr — chunked, compressed, n-dimensional array storage format for multidimensional scientific raster.
cogeo-mosaic — Python library for building quadkey-indexed MosaicJSON descriptors from COG collections.
DuckDB — in-process analytical SQL engine that reads Parquet and GeoParquet directly with predicate pushdown.
DuckDB httpfs extension — DuckDB extension enabling reads from HTTP and S3 URLs.
DuckDB spatial extension — DuckDB extension providing geometry types and ST_* spatial functions.
Entwine — toolkit for organising large point clouds into octree structures for streaming.
FastAPI — Python async web framework used by TiTiler and suggested for the CV inference proxy.
GDAL — Geospatial Data Abstraction Library; the universal raster and vector translation and processing library.
gdalbuildvrt — GDAL utility that builds a virtual raster mosaic over multiple source files.
gdal_translate — GDAL utility for raster format conversion, including the COG driver.
go-pmtiles — Go-based PMTiles HTTP server with S3 byte-range reads and ETag-driven cache invalidation.
PDAL — Point Data Abstraction Library for filtering, transforming, and converting LiDAR data.
rasterio — Python bindings over GDAL for reading and writing geospatial raster.
Samgeo — geospatial wrapper for Meta's Segment Anything Model that returns georeferenced GeoJSON detections.
Segment Anything Model (SAM) — Meta's foundation model for promptable image segmentation.
Tippecanoe (Felt fork) — vector tile generator producing PMTiles/MBTiles with geometry-aware simplification.
TiTiler — FastAPI dynamic tile server for COGs and MosaicJSON built on rasterio.
Valhalla — open-source routing engine using OSM tile hierarchies; supports route, isochrone, map-match.
Amazon API Gateway HTTP API — lightweight managed HTTP gateway supporting custom Lambda authorisers and VPC Link integrations.
Amazon Application Load Balancer (ALB v2) — Layer-7 load balancer with path-based listener rules, URL rewrite transforms, and IP/Lambda target groups.
Amazon Bedrock — managed service for accessing foundation models from multiple providers via a single API.
Amazon Bedrock AgentCore — Bedrock runtime for building multi-agent systems with session memory, tool use, and identity passthrough.
Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Base — managed RAG service over a vector store, exposing retrieval as a Bedrock API.
Amazon CloudFront — AWS content delivery network with per-behaviour cache policies and credential-header keying.
Amazon CloudWatch — AWS metrics, logs, and alarms service for operational observability.
Amazon CloudWatch Logs Insights — query language and engine for ad-hoc analysis of CloudWatch log groups.
Amazon Cognito User Pool — AWS-managed OIDC identity provider with hosted UI and federation support.
Amazon Cognito post-authentication Lambda trigger — Cognito hook invoked after successful sign-in, used here to convert invitations into platform memberships.
Amazon DynamoDB — AWS-managed key-value/document store with single-table design support, GSIs, conditional writes, and PITR.
Amazon DynamoDB Global Secondary Index (GSI) — alternate index over a DynamoDB table enabling inverse-direction or attribute-keyed queries.
Amazon DynamoDB Point-In-Time Recovery (PITR) — continuous DynamoDB backup with per-second restore points over the prior 35 days.
Amazon DynamoDB TTL — per-item time-to-live attribute that triggers asynchronous deletion when expired.
Amazon ECS Exec — mechanism to open an interactive shell into a running ECS task without SSH.
Amazon ECS Service Auto Scaling — auto-scaling for ECS services driven by CloudWatch metrics.
Amazon ElastiCache — managed in-memory cache service (Redis or Memcached engines).
Amazon EventBridge — AWS event bus and scheduler for inter-service events and cron-based Lambda invocations.
Amazon OpenSearch Serverless — on-demand OpenSearch with geo_point and geo_shape support for spatial and full-text queries.
Amazon S3 — AWS object storage with byte-range reads, versioning, lifecycle policies, and atomic CopyObject.
Amazon S3 CopyObject — atomic server-side object copy used here for live/staging swap of PMTiles archives.
Amazon S3 Glacier Deep Archive — lowest-cost S3 storage class with multi-hour retrieval time, used for long-term archive.
Amazon S3 Intelligent-Tiering — S3 storage class that auto-moves objects between access tiers based on usage.
Amazon S3 Lifecycle policy — prefix-scoped rules that transition or expire S3 objects on a schedule.
Amazon S3 Storage Lens — organisation-wide S3 storage analytics dashboard.
Amazon S3 Versioning — bucket-level setting that keeps non-current object versions for recovery.
Amazon SageMaker Async Inference — SageMaker endpoint mode supporting long-running queued GPU inference requests.
Amazon SES — managed email-sending service for notifications.
Amazon SNS — pub/sub messaging service supporting email, SMS, and HTTP delivery.
Amazon SQS — managed message queue used here to bridge S3 events into Step Functions, including dead-letter queues.
Amazon VPC Gateway Endpoints (S3, DynamoDB) — private route-table entries that allow VPC traffic to reach S3 and DynamoDB without an internet path.
AWS Batch — managed batch-job scheduler over ECS, Fargate, or EC2.
AWS Fargate — serverless container runtime for ECS tasks and services with per-task ephemeral storage up to 200 GiB.
AWS IAM — Identity and Access Management; policy-based access control for AWS resources.
AWS Lambda — serverless function runtime billed per invocation.
AWS Step Functions — managed workflow engine with state machines defined in Amazon States Language and visual execution history.
VPC Link for HTTP API — private connectivity layer between API Gateway HTTP API and VPC resources (such as an internal ALB).
CQL2 — OGC Common Query Language v2, text/JSON filter syntax for OGC API endpoints.
CRS84 and EPSG codes — coordinate reference system identifiers; CRS84 is the OGC alias for WGS84 lon/lat, EPSG codes index all CRSes.
CZML — JSON streaming format for time-dynamic 3D scene data, native to CesiumJS.
GTFS Realtime — protobuf-based feed format for live public-transit vehicle positions and alerts.
OGC API - Coverages — OGC standard for programmatic access to coverage (raster) data subsets.
OGC API - Features — OGC standard for HTTP/JSON access to feature collections.
OGC WMS 1.3.0 — Web Map Service; XML+image protocol for rendered map images, the legacy desktop-GIS standard.
OGC WMTS 1.0.0 — Web Map Tile Service; capabilities-driven tile delivery protocol consumed by QGIS and ArcGIS.
STAC 1.0.0 (SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog) — JSON schema and API for cataloguing geospatial assets.
STAC pointcloud extension — STAC extension describing point-cloud assets such as COPC files.
TileJSON — JSON descriptor for tile endpoints; URL template, bounds, zoom, attribution.
JSON Web Key Set (JWKS) (RFC 7517) — JSON document publishing an issuer's signing keys, used to verify JWT signatures.
JSON Web Token (JWT) (RFC 7519) — signed/encrypted token format used for OIDC bearer credentials.
Microsoft Entra ID — Microsoft's enterprise identity service (formerly Azure AD), an OIDC provider option.
OpenID Connect (OIDC) — identity layer over OAuth 2.0 producing signed JWT ID tokens.
OpenID Connect Discovery — standardised endpoint for discovering an OIDC provider's metadata; the basis for the trusted-issuers configuration.
Row-level security (RLS) — per-row access filtering applied transparently based on the requester's identity or claims. Closest canonical reference: PostgreSQL Row Security Policies.
RS256 / RS384 / RS512 (RFC 7518) — RSA SHA JWT signing algorithms accepted for token signature verification.
SHA-256 (NIST FIPS 180-4) — cryptographic hash used here to store API key fingerprints at rest.

h1: 6. Map clients, libraries, and frontend tools

ul.li: amazon-cognito-identity-js — JavaScript SDK for Cognito User Pool authentication flows.
CesiumJS — WebGL 3D globe and map engine supporting COPC, terrain, and CZML.
Esri VectorTileServer REST — Esri's proprietary REST contract for vector tile services consumed by ArcGIS Enterprise.
Form.io — offline-capable form runtime and builder, suggested for field-data capture.
GraphiQL — in-browser IDE for interactive GraphQL query construction with schema introspection.
MapLibre GL JS — open-source WebGL renderer for vector and raster maps, fork of Mapbox GL JS v1.
MapLibre Style Spec — JSON specification for declarative styling of vector and raster maps.
React — JavaScript library for component-based user interfaces; v19 with the React Compiler used in the map client.
React JSON Schema Form (RJSF) — React library that renders forms from JSON Schema definitions with client-side validation.
TerriaJS — open-source spatial-data explorer shell built on CesiumJS, originated by CSIRO Data61.
Vite — modern dev server and bundler for single-page-app builds.
Atomic S3 CopyObject swap — promotion pattern using S3's in-bucket copy to switch live artefacts without a half-written state.
Byte-range read (RFC 7233) — HTTP Range request returning a subset of an object's bytes; the substrate for COG/PMTiles/COPC.
ETag-driven cache refresh — pattern of invalidating an in-memory cache when an object's S3 ETag changes after an atomic swap.
Hive-style partitioning — directory convention key=value/... understood by analytical engines for partition-key pushdown.
Optimistic locking — concurrency control via a version attribute and conditional write, rejecting stale updates.
Predicate pushdown — engine pushes filter predicates down to the storage layer (Parquet row-group statistics, partition paths) to skip data.
Quadkey index — string encoding of tile coordinates used by MosaicJSON to index COGs by spatial cell.
Slowly Changing Dimension Type 2 (SCD2) — dimensional-modelling pattern that tracks change history with valid_from/valid_to/is_current columns. See Kimball Group: Type 2.
Tile coordinates (z/x/y) — Web Mercator slippy-map tiling scheme; the basis for XYZ tile URLs and PMTiles indexing.
Web Mercator (EPSG:3857) — projected CRS used by most web mapping tile pyramids.

p: These are the standards-compliant geospatial stacks worth being aware of when considering or extending this platform. Several were evaluated explicitly during the design — see Peer stacks and prior art in 16 Design Decisions.

ul.li: eoAPI — Development Seed's reference stack bundling pgSTAC, TiTiler, and tipg; the closest peer assembly to this platform. NASA IMPACT, MAAP, and VEDA build on it.
GeoNode — Django-based geospatial CMS layered on GeoServer.
GeoServer — Java-based OGC services server (WMS, WFS, WCS, WMTS, plus OGC APIs).

GeoServer Cloud — Spring Boot microservices refactor of GeoServer for cloud-native ECS/Kubernetes deployment. Trialled here and rejected for specific gaps (private-S3 COG reads, SOAP mosaic generation, UI friction); see Peer stacks and prior art for the full assessment.
ldproxy — Java OGC API server emphasising linked-data and HTML output.
MapProxy — Python tile-caching proxy for WMS/WMTS layers.
MapServer — long-standing C-based OGC web map server.
Martin — Rust vector-tile server backed by PostgreSQL/PostGIS or PMTiles; replaced here by go-pmtiles.
pg_featureserv — lightweight Go OGC API Features server over PostGIS.
pg_tileserv — lightweight Go vector tile server over PostGIS.
pgSTAC — PostgreSQL-backed STAC implementation used in this platform's prior iteration, since replaced.

pygeoapi — Python OGC API reference implementation (Features, Coverages, Tiles, Processes, EDR, Records). Did not (at the time of this design) provide GeoJSON feature editing over object-store-backed providers, which is the gap the editing pipeline fills.
stac-fastapi — Python/FastAPI STAC API server with multiple backend adapters (pgSTAC, OpenSearch, ElasticSearch).
tipg — Development Seed's OGC API Features and Tiles server over PostGIS.
titiler-pgstac — TiTiler variant that builds raster mosaics from STAC search backed by pgSTAC.
Amazon States Language (ASL) — JSON-based state-machine language used to define Step Functions workflows.
AWS CDK — AWS Cloud Development Kit; define cloud resources in code (Python, TypeScript, etc.) and compile to CloudFormation.
AWS CloudFormation — AWS declarative infrastructure-as-code service that CDK compiles to.
CfnListenerRule (CloudFormation) — CloudFormation primitive for ALB v2 path-based listener rules and URL-rewrite transforms.
GraphQL — typed, introspectable query language and runtime used for the platform's internal query layer.
HTTP 410 Gone (RFC 9110) — HTTP status indicating a resource is intentionally permanently removed; used for retired datasets.
OpenStreetMap (OSM) — open-licence global road network and feature database used to build Valhalla routing graphs.
RFC 3339 datetime — internet-friendly datetime format used by OGC API Features for temporal queries.

p: Suggestions, corrections, and broken links: this is the kind of reference page that needs occasional refresh. The canonical URLs were checked at publication; some will move. The descriptions are deliberately brief — each is a pointer, not an explanation.
